title: Stasis dermatitis review

referral.text: Supports documenting a current or healed venous leg ulcer and an already made vascular referral without generating diagnosis, referral, urgency, or treatment.
referral.rationale: Current or healed ulcer history and clinician-led referral context were reviewed; no diagnosis or referral is generated.
referral.evidence_extract: Supports documenting a current or healed venous leg ulcer and an already made vascular referral without generating diagnosis, referral, urgency, or treatment.

source.title: Varicose veins: diagnosis and management — Recommendations

exact_section.heading: Recommendation 1.2.2 — venous leg-ulcer history and referral context

follow_up.text: Supports documenting lower-limb pain, aching, discomfort, swelling, heaviness, itching, pigmentation, and eczema considered by the clinician in chronic venous insufficiency context.
follow_up.rationale: The exact venous-symptom and skin-change section supports documenting a stasis-dermatitis review, interval change, lower-limb pain, aching, discomfort, swelling, heaviness, itch, clinician skin inspection, distribution, pigmentation or eczema and impact without asserting chronic venous insufficiency.
follow_up.evidence_extract: Supports documenting lower-limb pain, aching, discomfort, swelling, heaviness, itching, pigmentation, and eczema considered by the clinician in chronic venous insufficiency context.

source.title: Varicose veins: diagnosis and management — Recommendations

exact_section.heading: Recommendation 1.2.2 — symptomatic veins and lower-limb skin changes

source_limitations: Laterality, exact skin findings, edema, varicosities, ulcer, pulses and arterial status, diagnosis, duplex, treatment, compression, medicine, referral, and follow-up remain unsupported.
Any legacy item not explicitly mapped to an exact section remains unsupported pending qualified clinician review.
No mapping authorises an automatic diagnosis, differential, investigation, management decision, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, urgency conclusion, discharge decision, or patient instruction.
All prior supported legacy-item mappings were removed because canonical item-level applicability and workflow-specific rationale were not present in explicit repository records.
curation_policy: Direct guideline curation: retain only explicitly mapped content; rewrite only with explicit item mapping; remove unmapped legacy content; add only committed exact-section evidence summaries.
